overview: Izzet Phoenix leverages discard and card draw to fuel Demilich, constantly replaying low-cost creatures from the graveyard. Manamorphose provides early acceleration and filtering, while Gut Shot offers efficient one-mana removal to clear blockers. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents through relentless board presence and card advantage before they can stabilize.
win_conditions: Attacking with continuously replayed low-cost creatures
Generating insurmountable card advantage through Demilich triggers
Clearing key threats with efficient removal like Gut Shot

keep_criteria: Hands with 2-3 lands and at least one low-cost creature
Hands with early interaction such as Gut Shot
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably duals or fetchable basics), at least one cheap cantrip (Gitaxian Probe, Opt, Manamorphose), and a discard outlet.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early creatures or draw engines
Hands composed entirely of high-cost cards
Mulligan if you have 0-1 lands, no cheap spells, or only high-cost threats.
In a 7-card mulligan, keep 3+ lands if you have multiple cheap spells or discard effects.

early_game: Play lands and cheap creatures to establish board presence
Cast Manamorphose to draw cards and filter into Demilich
Use Gut Shot to remove early aggressive threats
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Gitaxian Probe on your opponent's turn, or cast Manamorphose for mana/ramp.
Turn 2: Fetch a dual land, cast cheap cantrips to dig for Phoenix or discard, or sequence Manamorphose into a cheap spell.
Prioritize lands that enter untapped or can be fetched to ensure blue/red mana availability for Probe and Phoenix.
mid_game: Trigger Demilich to replay discarded creatures and maintain pressure
Cycle through the deck to find more removal and draw spells
Protect key engines while applying consistent damage
Prioritize graveyard consistency over board presence.
Keep cheap spells in hand to dig for Phoenix or disrupt, and avoid overcommitting to cantrips if you're low on life.
Manage mana carefully to ensure you can cast Probe/Phoenix on the correct turn without flooding the board.
Hold back threats until you have a clear path to cast Phoenix or have enough removal to handle interaction.
Commit when you have graveyard setup and can answer interaction, but avoid overextending into board wipes or sweepers.
late_game: Flood the board with replayed creatures to close out the game
Leverage card advantage to outvalue slower strategies
Clear remaining blockers with targeted removal to secure victory
Arclight Phoenix (cast for free or with Manamorphose), backed by spot removal and burn.
Gut Shot can finish off low-life opponents or trade efficiently in the late game.
When you have 2+ cards in the graveyard, can cast Probe/cheap spells, and the opponent has no graveyard hate or counterspells in play.
Go all-in if you can close the game in 1-2 turns or if the opponent is at lethal range.

key_cards.name: Manamorphose
key_cards.notes: Cast early to accelerate into Demilich or find removal; discard a card to draw two, fueling the graveyard engine.

key_cards.name: Gut Shot
key_cards.notes: Use to clear blockers or finish off damaged creatures; efficient one-mana removal that fits the deck's low mana curve.

key_cards.name: Demilich
key_cards.notes: Triggers on every discard to return a creature card to the battlefield; central to the deck's strategy of cycling and replaying threats.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize casting Manamorphose before Demilich to fuel the graveyard
Hold Gut Shot for key threats rather than using it preemptively
Discard creatures intentionally to trigger Demilich when mana is available
Common mistakes include overcommitting to cantrips without a win condition, ignoring graveyard hate, and mismanaging mana for Probe/Phoenix sequencing.
Manamorphose is crucial for mana fixing and ramp, while Gut Shot serves as niche burn/removal.
Demilich is highly atypical for Phoenix
if included, it likely changes the game to a graveyard-focused wincon, but be aware it's vulnerable to exile-based hate and may conflict with your core strategy.
Always track opponent's graveyard and hand size, and never assume your win condition will resolve without backup.

matchups.advice: Play around early pressure by holding up removal and replaying creatures to block
Apply early pressure before they stabilize and use card draw to outlast their resources
Race them by replaying creatures quickly and using Gut Shot to clear their value engines

Use Manamorphose for mana/ramp, cast Probe on the opponent's turn, use discard to remove counterspells, or cast Phoenix when they're tapped out. Sideboard Negate/Spell Pierce to protect your key spells.

Apply early pressure, use discard to hit their ramp sources, and close games before they stabilize. Gut Shot can finish off low-life ramp players, and Manamorphose helps you race their mana acceleration.

Win the discard war, control the graveyard, and out-value with better sequencing. Gut Shot can trade efficiently, and prioritizing Probe/cheap spells ensures you reach your win condition first. Track their graveyard to know when to hold up removal.

common_threats: Watch for graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Leyline, Surgical Extraction), counterspells, and discard.
Note if the opponent is playing fast lands or ramp to gauge if they can race you.
Track their hand size to know when they're tapped out for interaction.
